title: Legitimate Intervention
subtitle: Minimal Conditions for Reopening without Reclosure
core_thesis: Intervention is legitimate only when it reopens a closed default without destroying rollback, exception re-entry, or editability, and without installing replacement closure.
core_formula: I_legit iff Rb_p and Ex_p and Ed_p and not RC
responsibility_rule: Responsibility arises at the earliest non-action node where recognized closure, available authority, rollback availability, and withheld action coincide.
one_sentence: SDeltaPhi-32 distinguishes legitimate reopening from overwrite, coercive correction, and replacement closure.
negative_constraints: Do not treat intervention as automatically legitimate.
Do not treat declared moral intention as legitimacy.
Do not treat replacement closure as reopening.
Do not collapse all awareness into responsibility.
Do not use reserve-force case pattern to determine legal responsibility.
